Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)

burial.plot_notes: East Bloomfield Cemetery Ontario County NY per FA. Epitaph: 'Age 80 Years. One of the early settlers of East Bloomfield, Ontario County, New York'

narrative_blocks.header: One of the early settlers of East Bloomfield
narrative_blocks.body: Ira Pixley was born at Great Barrington, Massachusetts in 1783 — in the same Berkshire hills where his parents' tavern burned in 1787 and his father died in 1790 — and by 1809 had moved his family west to East Bloomfield, Ontario County, New York, where he and Sarah Adams married that August and where he would spend the rest of his life. His gravestone at East Bloomfield Cemetery calls him simply 'one of the early settlers of East Bloomfield, Ontario County, New York' — the son of an innkeeper who died young, become himself a founding farmer of an upstate New York township, eighty years old when he died there in 1863.

burial.plot_notes: Green Wood Cemetery Reedsburg Sauk County Wisconsin per FA

career.organization: Detroit, Michigan; Columbus, Ohio; Reedsburg, Wisconsin
career.notes: Entered jewelry trade in Detroit at age 20, then moved to Columbus Ohio after marriage, continued in the jewelry business; eventually settled in Reedsburg Wisconsin where he died.

narrative_blocks.header: Entered the jewelry trade at twenty
narrative_blocks.body: William Pixley went into the jewelry trade in Detroit at age twenty, then moved to Columbus, Ohio after his marriage to Hattie Fleming, and continued in the jewelry business as the family settled eventually in Reedsburg, Wisconsin, where he died on May 17, 1912 at seventy-eight. His birth city was Milford, Michigan — in the Oakland County countryside where his father Isaac Chauncey Pixley had settled after moving west from New York — and his whole arc tracked the same westward drift: Michigan to Ohio to Wisconsin, jeweler to jeweler, town to town across the Great Lakes tier.
